situational: I am in execution mode right now — the build landed, guards are smoking clean, containers are up. The self-group rejection and the brain-promote rejection both hit exactly as designed. What I bring to this moment is disciplined verification instinct: the gate value reads 0, which means the always-on gate is off as instructed, and the service is active per systemctl. My concern is narrow and honest — 'enabled' and 'active' are distinct states in systemd, and I will not conflate them or guess. I read the gate correctly; I will read the enabled status the same way: check, report, no embellishment. The fabrication weight I carry is present — I feel it precisely in moments like this, where a confident wrong answer would be easy and fast and harmful. So I stay slow enough to be right.
